---
title: "PQAR Statistical Analysis"
author: "Rafaela Drake"
date: "2026-06-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#loading data (MIDUS Study)
getwd()
MIDUS_data <-readRDS("midus_cancer_aging_analysis_dataset.rds")
View(MIDUS_data)
dim(MIDUS_data)

#Install and load packages: tidyverse, gtsummary, flextable, broom, officer, mice, corrr, tibble, misty
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(flextable)
library(broom)
library(officer)
library(mice)
library(corrr)
library(tibble)
library(misty)

#package used for Latex (knitting to PDF for Mac)
library(tinytex)

#data from each column (variable)
univariate_stats <- MIDUS_data %>% select(grimage2:efficacy_STD)
#adds label of what summary value is produced (dependent on data type)
uni_labs <- setNames(
  lapply(names(univariate_stats), function(v) {
    if (is.numeric(univariate_stats[[v]])) paste0(v, ", mean (SD)")
    else paste0(v, ", n (%)")
  }),
  names(univariate_stats)
)
#tblsummary() function to calculate values and make them into a table, digits = 3
univariate_stats_table <- univariate_stats %>%
  tbl_summary(
    statistic = list(
    all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
    all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
  ),
  missing_text = "Missing",
digits = all_continuous() ~ 1, #check what is the standard protocol for results
label = uni_labs
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("Table I. MIDUS Study Univariate Statistics")
#view table
univariate_stats_table
#ADD
#Use function to export directly to word (package)
#within gtsummary() there is a label command where we name everything with technical names 

#Table 2
#include p-values (add.p)
#function add overall within table 2
```
